display.purpose: The winning place name in the reel and result modal — the biggest, boldest type in the app.

headline.purpose: App title and sheet payoff headings.

title.purpose: Modal titles and the primary CTA label.

body.purpose: Input text, card content, the readable default.

label.purpose: Earned uppercase tracked caps — 'SPINNING…' and 'TODAY YOU'RE EATING AT' only.

shadows.name: card-rest
shadows.purpose: Ambient default lift for place cards and surfaces.

shadows.name: primary-glow
shadows.purpose: Signature colored orange glow under primary actions only — a 'tap this' promise.

motion.name: press
motion.purpose: Tactile press feedback on all buttons (scale(0.90) on small icon buttons).

motion.name: reel-decel
motion.purpose: Hand-authored roulette deceleration via requestAnimationFrame (not CSS keyframes).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one loud call-to-action — Pick Lunch, Add Place, submit.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #FF6B35; color: #fff; font-weight: 800; font-size: 1.25rem; padding: 20px 24px; border: none; border-radius: 24px; box-shadow: 0 4px 20px 0 rgba(255,107,53,0.35); transition: transform 0.1s ease; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.95); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Recessive companion to the primary — cancel, dismiss.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #6B7280; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1rem; padding: 14px 24px; border: 1px solid #E5E7EB; border-radius: 16px; transition: transform 0.1s ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: scale(0.95); }

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: Round 36px action on cards — favorite, exclude, edit, delete.
components.css: .ds-icon-btn { width: 36px; height: 36px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #F9FAFB; color: #FFD166; font-size: 1.125rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border: none; transition: transform 0.1s ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-icon-btn:active { transform: scale(0.90); }

components.name: Selector Chip
components.description: Price / distance toggle. Selected = orange fill; tap again to clear.
components.css: .ds-chip { flex: 1; padding: 10px 12px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; border-radius: 12px; background: #F9FAFB; color: #6B7280; border: 1px solid #E5E7EB; transition: background 0.15s, color 0.15s; cursor: pointer; } .ds-chip--on { background: #FF6B35; color: #fff; border-color: #FF6B35; }

components.name: Place Card
components.description: A saved spot. White on paper, soft shadow, state-colored hairline border.
components.html:
★

The Pizza Place

Italian · Picked 3 times
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; border-radius: 16px; padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 2px 16px 0 rgba(26,26,46,0.08); border: 1px solid #FDE68A; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-card-head { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; } .ds-star { color: #FBBF24; } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1A1A2E; } .ds-card-meta { margin: 0; font-size: 0.75rem; color: #6B7280; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Form field with orange focus ring; 16px text to prevent iOS zoom.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="e.g. The Pizza Place" />
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; padding: 12px 16px; font-size: 1rem; color: #1A1A2E; background: #F9FAFB; border: 1px solid #E5E7EB; border-radius: 12px; outline: none; transition: box-shadow 0.15s, border-color 0.15s; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #9CA3AF; } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: #FF6B35; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #FF6B35; }

narrative.overview: Lunch Roulette holds one belief: the screen stays calm until it has a reason not to. A warm off-white canvas, one loud orange call-to-action, a clean list of your spots — that's the resting state, almost boring in its restraint. Then you tap, the reel spins, and the whole app erupts into a color-banded, confetti-firing payoff. All the energy is concentrated in that one burst. Design serves speed — open, spin, answer in under five seconds — and playfulness rides on top of that speed as color, emoji, and springy motion, never as decoration that slows the user down.
keyCharacteristics: Calm canvas, loud payoff — energy is concentrated, not spread evenly.
One primary action, always the loudest thing on screen.
Bright warm accents (orange primary, yellow + green support) on an off-white base.
Chunky radii and springy, tactile motion — bouncy, never stiff.
Emoji as functional garnish, not filler.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Orange is the only call-to-action color. Almost never more than one primary orange element per view; its scarcity makes the Pick Lunch button impossible to miss.

rules.name: The Celebration-Only Gradient Rule
rules.body: The orange→yellow→green gradient is reserved for the win moment. It never appears as background chrome, dividers, or button fills. Seeing the gradient means you got an answer.

rules.name: The Earned Caps Rule
rules.body: Uppercase tracked type appears in exactly two places, both tied to the spin ceremony. A spotlight, never a scaffold — never a tracked-uppercase kicker above an ordinary section.

rules.name: The Colored-Glow-Means-Go Rule
rules.body: Only primary orange actions get the orange glow. Neutral surfaces get neutral ink shadows. The glow is a promise: tap this.

dos: Do keep orange as the one voice — one primary orange action per view, backed by its Primary Glow (0 4px 20px rgba(255,107,53,0.35)).
Do keep the resting canvas calm (Warm Paper #FFFBF5, white cards, one CTA) so the spin and result read as an eruption by contrast.
Do reserve the orange→yellow→green gradient and falling emoji for the win moment only.
Do give every interactive element tactile press feedback (active:scale-95 / -90) and spring entrances — bouncy is the brand.
Do use round radii at scale: 24px primary buttons, 16px cards, 12px inputs/chips, full-round icon buttons.
Do keep muted gray (#6B7280) on white or paper only, where it holds ~4.5:1; honor prefers-reduced-motion by crossfading to the result instead of running the reel.
donts: Don't build a corporate SaaS dashboard — no enterprise gray fields, dense tables, or settings-panel density.
Don't let the roulette drift into cheap-casino territory — no neon, no slot-machine chrome, no coin-shower kitsch.

Don't ship the generic AI-template look: no cream/sand body tint dressed up as 'warmth,' no tracked-uppercase eyebrow over ordinary sections, no identical icon-heading-text card grids, no gradient text.
Don't clutter like a food-delivery app — no promo banners, ad strips, or ratings noise.
Don't use border-left/border-right colored stripes as accents; borders here signal state (favorite/excluded) with full hairline rings only.
Don't give neutral or secondary buttons the orange glow — the colored shadow is reserved for the primary action.
